summary: Iter 78ab LP SmartSide AI formula preview tested end-to-end. Backend: 12 new HTTP tests for /api/admin/lp-formula-preview + /presets all pass (preset catalog, token gating, response shape, lap +9.1%, shake +329%, 190 Series batten emission, invalid/empty body 400, flag default OFF). Regression: 57 existing tests across test_lp_smartside_formulas.py + test_profile_siding_lines.py + test_j_channel_formula.py + test_gutter_geometry.py also pass. Frontend: /lp-formula-preview shield page, token-gated load, all 4 presets in dropdown, STAGED (OFF) banner, summary tiles (28 lines / 6 changed), 6 highlighted rows visible. Live diff verified: 38 Series Lap 264→288 PCS (+24, +9.1%), Shake 44→189 PCS (+145, +329.5%), 190 Series Trim 19/32" x 3" x 16' 0→11 PCS (+11) — matches PDF math expectations. Preset switch (campbell→shake_heavy) updates table (28→23 rows). Vero base-included popover visually verified on EST-658882-W (Climatech Plus glass / White frame / Full Flex Screen / Tilt-out sashes).
critical_code_review_comments: routes/lp_admin.py — `_OVERRIDE_FLAG` is a module-level global. Comment correctly notes thread-unsafety. In FastAPI under uvicorn workers this is fine for single-request async use but a stray concurrent admin preview call could clobber the flag. Consider using a contextvars.ContextVar for true per-request isolation.

routes/lp_admin.py — the endpoint re-runs `_build_lines` twice in the same request (once with flag OFF, once ON). On large HOVER payloads this doubles the work; acceptable for an admin preview but worth a `# noqa: perf` comment.

lp_smartside_formulas.py — `NICKEL_GAP_J_CHANNEL_NEEDS_REVIEW = True` is dead code marked with `noqa: F841`. Howard's Q3=C says 'keep in memory' but a constant assigned and never read is brittle. Consider a TODO module docstring instead.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on LP preview UI; Vero base-includes popover visually verified; Vero 8-adder dropdown and Mezzo Aluminum 1/2 Screen popover NOT programmatically asserted (DOM probes returned empty — popover content renders correctly per screenshot but my selectors via [role=tooltip]/[role=dialog] missed the actual portal). Main agent should re-verify with the correct popover wrapper class if a strict programmatic assertion is required.

test_credentials: Admin hhunt6677@yahoo.com / Admin123! (POST /api/auth/login → 200). Supplier admin token OXSp1EXqp1rPLsQfeEoZyDbFCLZ3D6B2D55HyO1LFoE accepted by /api/admin/lp-formula-preview.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing EST-658882-W and the 4 built-in presets (campbell/shake_heavy/bb_heavy/lap_only).
context_for_next_testing_agent: LP preview admin endpoint is solid. Live numbers from POST /api/admin/lp-formula-preview {preset: campbell}: 28 LP lines, 6 changed; Shake row pdf_qty/legacy_qty ≈ 4.3× (189 vs 44, +329.5%); 38 Series Lap 8" jumped 264→288 PCS (+9.1%); 190 Series Trim 19/32" x 3" x 16' goes 0→11 PCS when B&B present; 38 Series Vertical Panel goes 6.2→0 PCS (-100%) because the PDF formula moves that area to the dedicated 4'x10' Panel SKU (legacy 6.2 → 6 PCS migrates onto '38 Series 4' x 10' Panel' 0→6 PCS, +100%). All 4 presets respond <500ms. flag_currently_enabled correctly returns false (LP_AI_FORMULAS_V1 stays OFF in env). Vero/Mezzo regression: 635 vero-* and 7 mezzo-* testids are emitted on EST-658882-W; vero-base-included popover content visually verified in screenshot. Use the popover wrapper class (NOT [role=tooltip]) for programmatic assertion next time — the content is rendered but uses a custom popover container without a role attribute.
rca of the issue: No blocking issue. The LP formula preview is correctly computing the legacy-vs-PDF diff inside a single request via lp_formulas.override_flag() context manager without mutating env. Diff math matches Howard's expected behaviour: Shake 9.09 sqft/PCS legacy → 2.33 sqft/PCS PDF (7" reveal) yields ~4× increase; lap goes from 9.09 → 9.17 sqft/PCS (slight bump); B&B batten line newly emitted via shake_540_series_bump + board_batten_batten_pieces. Feature flag is OFF in production as required (STAGED banner visible).
